commit.message:
Print proxy configuration on startup if used (#1089)

When a proxy is configured, there was no visibility into the active
proxy settings at server startup, making it difficult to confirm proxy
is picked up correctly.

## Changes

- **`server/server.js`**: Imports `getProxyConfig` and logs proxy
details after `configCache` is initialized (so `platform.json` values
are resolved alongside env vars)
- Logs `🌐 Proxy configuration active` with `http`, `https`, `noProxy`,
and `urlPatterns` when a proxy URL is present
  - Logs `Proxy is explicitly disabled` when `enabled: false` is set
  - Silent when no proxy is configured at all

## Example output

```json
{"component":"Server","level":"info","message":"🌐 Proxy configuration active","http":"(not set)","https":"http://proxy.example.com:8080","noProxy":"localhost,127.0.0.1","urlPatterns":"(all URLs proxied)"}
```

commit.message:
Fix SARIF formatter silently dropping findings and missing locations (#393)

The SARIF formatter only iterated PackageDependencies when collecting
findings, causing all findings from BuildDependencies to be silently
omitted. Additionally, two rego rules produced findings with purls that
could never match a package in the formatter's purl-based lookup:

- github_action_from_unverified_creator_used used the action purl as the
  finding identifier and emitted coarse "Used in N repo(s)" aggregates
  with no file path or line number, resulting in empty SARIF locations.
  Rewritten to emit per-step findings with path, line, job, step, and
  event triggers, keyed by pkg.purl.

- known_vulnerability_in_build_platform used input.provider (e.g.
  "gitlab") as the finding purl. Changed to use pkg.purl so findings
  are discoverable by the formatter.

The formatter now iterates both PackageDependencies and
BuildDependencies for purl lookup, with deduplication via a seen set,
and avoids append into a foreign slice's backing array.

Fixes #390

Signed-off-by: Mikaël Barbero <mikael.barbero@eclipse-foundation.org>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix solver bug: active count was halved after each append

After appending, the board gains live.length NEW cells on top of
the existing live.length active cells = live.length * 2 total.
Setting active = live.length (not *2) caused the solver to
declare victory when only half the cells were cleared, accepting
boards that are genuinely unsolvable.

commit.message:
fix: prevent stack overflow in Test262 CI with deep recursion

- Reduce MAX_CALL_STACK_DEPTH from 256 to 64 so the interpreter raises
  a JavaScript RangeError well before the native stack is exhausted.
  Each interpreter frame can consume several KiB of native stack space,
  and 64 levels is sufficient for any realistic JavaScript program.

- Add the stacker crate and wrap Interpreter::run() with
  stacker::maybe_grow(128KiB, 2MiB) so the native stack is dynamically
  extended via mmap/VirtualAlloc when headroom drops below 128 KiB.
  This is the same technique used by rustc itself and prevents
  SIGSEGV/STATUS_STACK_OVERFLOW on pathological inputs (e.g. Test262
  JSON.parse nesting tests, Proxy trap recursion).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: restore AI chat responses by fixing API key resolution

- Add EXPO_PUBLIC_OPENAI_API_KEY support so key is available in
  production builds on Vercel (where .env.local doesn't exist)
- Update app.config.js to check EXPO_PUBLIC_ prefixed env var first
- Replace silent template fallback with thrown errors so the UI
  shows clear error messages instead of fake responses
- Add console.error logging when API key is missing
- Show descriptive error messages in chat UI when AI fails

Root cause: On Vercel's build servers, .env.local is absent so
process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY is undefined. app.config.js evaluated
it as undefined, making Constants.expoConfig.extra.openaiApiKey
also undefined. All AI responses silently fell back to hardcoded
template text with no error indication.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci(gh-aw): trigger fix-build-failure only on update failure

Switch from workflow_run (fires on every completion) to
workflow_dispatch, triggered explicitly by update.yml on failure.
This prevents the fix-build-failure workflow from running when the
update succeeds, avoiding unnecessary agent invocations.

commit.message:
consensus: Increase max block size from 1MB to 2MB

Block 14075 (1,033,883 bytes) was rejected during sync because it
slightly exceeded the 1MB limit. This block was already accepted by
the consensus majority. Blocks with many transactions can legitimately
exceed 1MB, and the wire frame limit is 8MB, so 2MB is a safe increase.

Updated constants.rs, server.rs size checks, and related tests.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: filter masternodes not in consensus from peer pool

During peer discovery, after health-checking all candidates, compute the
highest block height among healthy peers. Any peer lagging more than
CONSENSUS_LAG (3) blocks behind is dropped from the pool and a warning is
logged. Because the existing failover logic compares the active peer against
the returned pool, a currently-active peer that falls out of consensus is
automatically replaced on the next discovery cycle.

Fix other db support for users/cont3xt/cache (#3782)

* Fix other db support for users/cont3xt/cache

Added support for sqlite and fixed some bugs with lmdb/redis

   Common:
   - Added SQLite backend for User (common/user.js)
   - Fixed searchUsers pagination when from/size are undefined (LMDB, Redis, SQLite)
   - Fixed User.setUser to return Promise when no callback provided
   - Fixed makeRegressionUser async/await chain
   - Fixed ES deleteAllUsers race condition (conflicts: proceed, refresh: true)
   - Added SQLite cache backend to arkimeCache.js
   - Removed Redis cache old format (redisFormat=2) conversion code

   Cont3xt:
   - Added SQLite and Redis implementations to cont3xt/db.js
   - Added /regressionTests/clearAll endpoint for all backends
   - Sort views by name in apiGet for deterministic ordering

   Viewer:
   - Updated checkURLs to accept non-http schemes (sqlite, lmdb, redis)
   - Derive users URL from options.node when options.url is absent

   Tests:
   - Added mini-redis.pl with KEYS, SETNX, SHUTDOWN commands and --debug flag
   - Added audit date range/pagination/sort tests to cont3xt.t
   - Added USERSELASTICSEARCH env var support for multi-backend testing
   - Fixed mini-redis shutdown reliability in tests.pl

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refresh before deleting everything

* get otherdbtest working

* need to include path

* Fix otherdbtest redis port to use mini-redis on 7379

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Multi-backend notifier, parliament, and data isolation fixes

- Rewrite notifier.js with ES/LMDB/Redis/SQLite implementations
  using User.getDbUrl() to select backend automatically
- Rewrite Parliament class with ES/LMDB/Redis/SQLite implementations
- Add User.getDbUrl() static method to common/user.js
- Add searchNotifiers({name, userId, roles, all}) interface replacing
  raw ES queries; sort by created then name for deterministic order
- Add deleteAllNotifiers and /regressionTests/deleteAllNotifiers endpoint
- Normalize getNotifier to return {id, ...doc} or null across all backends
- Simplify API methods to single try/catch, consistent error messages

- Fix LMDB data isolation: createLMDBStore now uses openDB(section) to
  create named sub-databases so User/Notifier/Parliament don't share keys
- Fix Redis data isolation: add user: prefix to Redis user keys so
  deleteAllUsers doesn't wipe notifier:/parliament: keys
- Add ArkimeUtil.createSQLiteDB() standardizing URL parsing and pragmas
  (WAL, synchronous=NORMAL, busy_timeout=5000) across all SQLite users

- Add parliament.t and api-notifiers.t to CI otherdbtest runs
- Start mini-redis.pl before viewer/parliament/cont3xt processes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Cache improvements: name-based key isolation, BSON error handling, SQLite expiry

- Add configurable name prefix to cache backends so multiple caches
  sharing the same Redis/Memcached/SQLite storage do not collide:
  Redis/Memcached use cache:{name}: key prefix, SQLite uses
  cache_{name} table name. Pass name from cont3xt and wise.

- Wrap BSON.deserialize in try/catch in Redis, Memcached, and SQLite
  cache backends so corrupted entries return undefined (cache miss)
  instead of crashing.

- Add 1-minute cleanup timer in SQLite cache to delete expired entries.
  Timer uses unref() so it does not prevent Node process exit.

- Guard updateLocalShortcuts against null usersClient7 when users
  backend is non-ES (Redis/LMDB/SQLite), preventing error spam in
  the viewer log.

- Add timestamps to mini-redis debug output and start mini-redis with
  --debug flag when tests run in debug mode, logging to /tmp/arkime.redis.

- Add echo labels (LMDB/SQLITE/REDIS) to CI build.yml otherdb test
  steps for clearer CI output.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix typo

* Rewrite mini-redis with non-blocking I/O to fix flaky Redis tests

The old mini-redis used blocking reads in parse_command/read_line,
so a client sending a partial RESP message would block the entire
single-threaded server, starving all other clients and causing
request timeouts in the test suite.

Rewritten to use O_NONBLOCK sockets with sysread into per-connection
buffers. parse_command is now a pure function that returns a parsed
command only when the buffer contains a complete RESP message.
Also added CLIENT command handler and Listen backlog of 128.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

FIXES (Code Review Issues):

1. **Metadata creation methods now throw descriptive errors on missing headers**
   - createEntityDefinition, createAttribute, createRelationship, createGlobalOptionSet
   - Previously returned empty strings silently when OData-EntityId header missing
   - Now throw explicit errors: "Failed to retrieve MetadataId from response. The OData-EntityId header was missing."
   - Guides users to identify real issues (network failures, API changes, etc.)

2. **GET_ATTRIBUTE_ODATA_TYPE IPC handler now enforces type safety**
   - Added runtime enum validation against AttributeMetadataType values
   - Removed unsafe `as any` cast that bypassed TypeScript's type system
   - Throws descriptive error listing all valid types when invalid value provided
   - Example: "Invalid attribute type: 'InvalidType'. Valid types are: String, Integer, Boolean, ..."

TECHNICAL DISCOVERY:

**Dataverse metadata operations return HTTP 204 No Content with NO response body**:
- Standard operations (entity, attribute, relationship, global option set): 204 No Content
- Only OData-EntityId header contains the created MetadataId
- Empty response body → makeHttpRequest returns `response.data = {}`
- Exception: CreateCustomerRelationships action returns 200 OK with JSON body containing AttributeId and RelationshipIds

This discovery led to abandoning a proposed "fallback to response body" approach, as responseData["MetadataId"] would always be undefined for metadata operations.

1. **execute() method**: Added comprehensive JSDoc with examples for:
   - CreateCustomerRelationships action (customer lookup creation with 200 OK response)
   - InsertStatusValue action (status choice column values with StateCode)

   - Bound vs unbound actions/functions

2. **queryData() method**: Added examples demonstrating:
   - Retrieving global option sets by name: `GlobalOptionSetDefinitions(Name='name')`
   - Retrieving all global option sets with filters
   - Retrieving by MetadataId

3. **createPolymorphicLookupAttribute()**: Added note about CreateCustomerRelationships alternative

4. **insertOptionValue()**: Added note about InsertStatusValue for status choice columns

5. **createGlobalOptionSet()**: Added retrieval example using queryData()

BENEFITS:

- **Fail-Fast Approach**: Clear errors replace silent failures, improving debuggability
- **Type Safety**: Runtime enum validation prevents invalid API calls

* feat(dataverse): add whitelist-based header validation for metadata operations

Implemented comprehensive security validation for custom headers in metadata
operations to prevent header injection attacks and API compliance issues.

SECURITY ENHANCEMENTS:
- Added validateMetadataHeaders() method with whitelist-based validation
- Validates all custom headers against allowed list from Microsoft documentation
- Blocks attempts to override protected headers (Authorization, Content-Type, etc.)
- Case-insensitive header matching per HTTP specification (RFC 2616)
- Detailed error messages showing invalid headers and allowed alternatives

ALLOWED CUSTOM HEADERS (per Microsoft Dataverse Web API docs):
- MSCRM.SolutionUniqueName: Associates metadata changes with solutions
- MSCRM.MergeLabels: Controls label merging behavior (true/false)
- Consistency: Forces reading latest version (value: "Strong")
- If-Match: Standard HTTP header for optimistic concurrency control
- If-None-Match: Standard HTTP header for caching control

PROTECTED HEADERS (never allowed in customHeaders):
- Authorization, Accept, Content-Type, OData-MaxVersion, OData-Version,
  Prefer, Content-Length (all controlled by makeHttpRequest)

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Created ALLOWED_METADATA_HEADERS constant with whitelist (5 headers)
- Created PROTECTED_HEADERS constant with blacklist (7 headers)
- Integrated validation into buildMetadataHeaders() for automatic coverage
- All metadata operations now automatically validated:
  * createEntityDefinition / updateEntityDefinition
  * createAttribute / updateAttribute
  * createRelationship / updateRelationship
  * createGlobalOptionSet / updateGlobalOptionSet
  * createPolymorphicLookup

BENEFITS:
- Prevents header injection attacks and accidental header overrides
- Ensures API compliance with Microsoft's documented patterns
- Provides clear developer feedback when invalid headers are used
- Defense-in-depth validation even for type-safe inputs

DOCUMENTATION REFERENCES:
Based on thorough review of 6 Microsoft Learn articles covering all metadata
operation types (entity, attribute, relationship, option set operations).

Changes maintain backward compatibility - all existing code uses valid headers
through typed MetadataOperationOptions interface.

Add new getCSDLDocument() method to DataverseAPI that retrieves the complete
CSDL/EDMX metadata document from Dataverse's OData $metadata endpoint.

Key Features:
- Returns raw XML containing complete schema metadata (entities, attributes,
  relationships, actions, functions, complex types, enum types)
- Supports gzip/deflate compression for optimal transfer (70-80% size reduction)
- Automatic decompression handling using Node.js zlib module
- Multi-connection support (primary/secondary) for advanced tools
- Response size: 1-5MB typical, up to 10MB+ for complex environments

Implementation:
- Added GET_CSDL_DOCUMENT IPC channel to channels.ts
- Implemented getCSDLDocument() in DataverseManager with:
  - Custom HTTPS request handler (avoids JSON parsing)
  - Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate headers
  - Automatic decompression via zlib.gunzip/inflate
  - Buffer-based response handling for binary data
- Registered IPC handler in index.ts with connection targeting support
- Exposed method in toolPreloadBridge.ts and preload.ts
- Added comprehensive TypeScript definitions in dataverseAPI.d.ts

Use Cases:
- Build Dataverse REST Builder (DRB) clone tools
- Create intelligent query builders and code generators
- Generate TypeScript interfaces from schema
- Explore entity relationships and metadata
- Validate action/function parameters

Naming Rationale:
Method named getCSDLDocument() (not getMetadata()) to avoid confusion with
existing entity metadata operations like getEntityMetadata() and
getAllEntitiesMetadata(). CSDL (Common Schema Definition Language) clearly
indicates it returns the OData service document.

Related: #384

* fix: expose getCSDLDocument on window.dataverseAPI for direct tool access

Fixed issue where getCSDLDocument was only accessible via
window.toolboxAPI.dataverse.getCSDLDocument but not directly on
window.dataverseAPI.getCSDLDocument like other Dataverse operations.

Changes:
- Added getCSDLDocument to toolPreloadBridge.ts dataverseAPI namespace
- Now consistent with other operations (getSolutions, queryData, etc.)
- Tools can call dataverseAPI.getCSDLDocument() directly as expected

This ensures getCSDLDocument follows the same exposure pattern as all
other Dataverse API methods.

* fix: decompress error responses in getCSDLDocument for readable error messages

Previously, error responses (non-200 status codes) were converted directly
from Buffer to UTF-8 string without checking for compression. Since the
request includes 'Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate' header, Dataverse may
return compressed error responses, resulting in garbled error messages.

Changes:
- Added decompression logic to error response path
- Check content-encoding header (gzip/deflate/none)
- Decompress error body before converting to string
- Added try-catch to handle decompression failures gracefully
- Error messages now readable regardless of compression

This mirrors the success path's decompression logic and ensures consistent
handling of both success and error responses.

* Fix launch prevention by migrating version fields to installed tool manifests

ROOT CAUSE IDENTIFIED: Installed tool manifest.json files don't have minAPI/maxAPI fields.
When tools load for launch, they read from manifest without version info, causing
isToolSupported() to return COMPATIBLE as "legacy tool".

SOLUTION: Add manifest migration in loadTool():
- Check if installed manifest is missing version fields
- Fetch version info from Supabase registry
- Update local manifest.json with minAPI/maxAPI
- Tool will now correctly show as INCOMPATIBLE and prevent launch

NEW METHODS:
- toolRegistryManager.getToolFromRegistry() - fetch tool from Supabase
- toolRegistryManager.updateInstalledManifest() - update local manifest

CLEANUP:
- Removed all debug logging from VersionManager.isToolSupported()

This fix ensures all installed tools (even those installed before version checking
was implemented) will have their compatibility correctly determined.

Co-authored-by: Power-Maverick <36135520+Power-Maverick@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix launch prevention by clearing tool cache on uninstall

ROOT CAUSE: Tools were remaining in memory cache after uninstall. When reinstalled
with updated manifest (including minAPI/maxAPI), the old cached tool object (without
version fields) was reused, causing isToolSupported() to be called with undefined
values and return COMPATIBLE.

FIX: Clear tool from cache when uninstalled. This ensures that on reinstall, the
tool is reloaded from the new manifest with correct version information.

REVERTED: Previous manifest migration commit (9713490) - was unnecessary and
incorrect approach.

CLEANUP: Removed all debug logging from VersionManager.isToolSupported()

This fix ensures tools properly show "Not Supported" badge and prevent launch when
incompatible, even after uninstall/reinstall cycles.

* Fix bug where Restart & Install Now does nothing (#427)

* Initial plan

* fix: Restart & Install Now sends install channel after download-in-modal transition

Root cause: CONFIG is JSON-serialized at modal creation with type:"available".
When a download completes while the "available" modal is still open, the UI
switches to show "Restart & Install Now" but CONFIG.type remains "available",
so clicking the button sent the `download` channel again (re-triggering a
download) instead of the `install` channel (calling quitAndInstall).

Fix:
- Introduce mutable `effectiveType` in controller.ts (init from CONFIG.type,
  updated to "downloaded" on update:downloaded message). All click/dismiss
  logic uses effectiveType instead of CONFIG.type.
- Add setErrorState() helper and handle update:error message so failures
  surface in the modal rather than silently disabling the button.
- In autoUpdateManagement.ts, catch downloadUpdate/quitAndInstall failures
  and forward them to the modal via sendBrowserWindowModalMessage.
- Forward onUpdateError events to the modal when it is open so
  autoUpdater-level errors (e.g. missing installer path) are also shown.

commit.message:
fix: restore MCP servers and skills when recreating expired session (#330)

## Problem
When a session's JSON-RPC connection is lost and the server reports
"Session not found" (session expired), the reconnect path in
`SendPromptAsync` creates a fresh `SessionConfig` that was missing
`McpServers` and `SkillDirectories`. This caused the "environment to
keep going away" — MCP tools and skills disappeared after reconnection.

## Root Cause
In `CopilotService.cs`, the `freshConfig` in the "Session not found"
catch block only set `Model`, `WorkingDirectory`, `Tools`, and
`OnPermissionRequest`. The original `CreateSessionAsync()` also includes
`McpServers` and `SkillDirectories` loaded from settings.

## Fix
Updated the reconnect path's `freshConfig` to load `McpServers` and
`SkillDirectories` from settings, matching the original
`CreateSessionAsync` behavior.

## Tests
Added 3 structural regression tests in `ConnectionRecoveryTests.cs`:
- `SendPromptAsync_FreshSessionConfig_IncludesMcpServers`
- `SendPromptAsync_FreshSessionConfig_IncludesSkillDirectories`
- `SendPromptAsync_FreshSessionConfig_MatchesCreateSessionFields`

All 34 ConnectionRecoveryTests pass. Build succeeds on Mac Catalyst.

---------

commit.message:
fix: prevent stack overflow in Test262 runner

- Increase test262 thread stack from 64 MiB to 128 MiB
- Reduce MAX_CALL_STACK_DEPTH from 512 to 256 to limit native stack
  consumption from deeply recursive JavaScript code
- Add push_call_frame/pop_call_frame guards to dispatch_call_value and
  dispatch_call_with_this which previously bypassed depth checking,
  allowing unbounded recursion through Function.prototype.call/apply

commit.message:
feat: add H149 IFD chain cycle detection and H150 tile geometry validation

H149 (CWE-835): Walks raw IFD next-pointers to detect circular references
that would cause infinite loops in libtiff's TIFFReadDirectory. Tracks
visited offsets in a set; flags cycles and excessive chain depth (>1024).

H150 (CWE-122/CWE-131): Validates tiled TIFF geometry — tile dimensions
must be multiples of 16 (TIFF 6.0 §15), tile counts must match expected
grid layout, tile byte counts checked for integer overflow and EOF overrun.

Registry updated: 148 → 150 heuristics (kTotalHeuristics auto-derived).
Tests: 230/230 pass, 0 ASAN/UBSAN errors.

fix(bundles): use relative paths for intra-monorepo bundle references

Replace unresolvable namespace refs (ado-pr:bundle, ado-scrum:bundle, etc.)
in azure-devops/bundle.md with relative file paths so sub-bundles are loaded

Also fix non-standard extends/behaviors keys in ado-pr and ado-research bundles.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: set SatelliteResourceLanguages=en to prevent FileNotFoundException on startup

When SatelliteResourceLanguages includes non-English cultures (fr/de/nl), the
Android runtime expects those satellite DLLs to exist (e.g. fr/System.Private.CoreLib.resources.dll).
These DLLs do not exist for all .NET runtime assemblies, causing FileNotFoundException.

Setting it to 'en' only makes the runtime fall back to neutral (English) resources
for all cultures gracefully. Our app handles its own localization via LocalizationService.
